target.title: feat(cloudflare): advertise FeatureRunSession + return run-session handle
target.body:
Implements the Cloudflare provider slice of the run-session adoption tracker #182.

## What changed
- Advertise `core.FeatureRunSession` for `cloudflare`.
- Return a `RunSessionHandle` from every post-identity run path.
- Keep `RunID` empty because Cloudflare has a lease identity but no distinct execution-run identity.
- Emit a shell-quoted cleanup command: `crabbox stop --provider cloudflare --id <lease-id>`.
- Add keep, keep-on-failure, cleanup quoting, provider-spec, docs, and changelog coverage.

## Live Cloudflare proof
Validated against a deployed Cloudflare Container Worker with an ephemeral bearer secret, removed immediately after the test:

```sh
crabbox run \
  --provider cloudflare \
  --type lite \
  --keep \
  --lease-output /tmp/cloudflare-session.json \
  --no-sync \
  --timing-json \
  -- true
```

Observed redacted handle:

```json
{
  "provider": "cloudflare",
  "leaseId": "<redacted>",
  "slug": "<redacted>",
  "reused": false,
  "kept": true,
  "cleanupCommand": "crabbox stop --provider cloudflare --id '<redacted>'"
}
```

`runId` was absent as intended. Executing the emitted cleanup command stopped the exact retained container successfully. Afterward the temporary Worker secret was deleted and readiness returned to its original `503 runner token is not configured` state.

## Verification
- `go test -race ./internal/providers/cloudflare`
- `go test ./internal/cli -run 'Test.*(LeaseOutput|RunLease|RunSession|Cloudflare)' -count=1`
- `go vet ./internal/providers/cloudflare ./internal/cli`
- Codex autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Maintainer fixup: 2c4a3e25.

Thanks @zozo123.
